bc



Suite: bc (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 ClockSync tests failed because Kubernetes log streaming to linuxptp-daemon pods returned context deadline exceeded / timeout errors during the GetProfileLogID step, preventing the test from locating the ptp4l config log line needed to derive the foreign master clock ID.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Kubernetes pod log stream failure in GetProfileLogID (affects: all 3 tests — "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-hrsj8 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=timedout waiting for matches


could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-fpgx7 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

From the detailed agent logs (prior identical run same day, pod linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg):

```

[ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 1 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...

[ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 2 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...

[ptptesthelper.go: 68] GetClockIDForeign retry due to err: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

Additionally, the API server was returning Unauthorized by 08:29 UTC (end of job), with the bc ClockSync tests running at 07:02–08:29 UTC — indicating progressive auth/credential degradation throughout the long 3.5-hour job:

```

error: You must be logged in to the server (Unauthorized)

E0624 08:29:47.118913 memcache.go:265] "Unhandled Error" err="couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials"

```

• Root Cause: The test helper GetProfileLogID in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go opens a live Kubernetes log stream (Follow: true, no TailLines) via pods.GetPodLogsRegex searching for the regex (?m).?Ptp4lConf: #profile: (.|\n)?message_tag \[([^\]]*)\] within a 3-minute timeout. The stream — opened with context.TODO() so no test-side deadline — is being terminated by the infrastructure with context deadline exceeded before the regex can be matched. This error pattern repeats identically across all ClockSync tests in bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, and dualnicbcha suites, confirming a cluster-wide log streaming connectivity issue rather than a daemon log format regression. The linuxptp-daemon does emit this log line (printNodeProfile in pkg/daemon/daemon.go:1369 writes the rendered Ptp4lConf with #profile: prefix and message_tag), but the HTTP connection carrying the pod log stream is dropped before the data reaches the test. The progressive Unauthorized failures at job end suggest the kubeconfig token was near expiry, which may also have disrupted API server proxy connections to node kubelets mid-run.
• Breaking PR/Commit:


TL;DR: Cluster log-stream collapse + API auth expiry wiped 22 of 24 tests; a linuxptp-daemon state-machine regression caused the remaining 2 TGM holdover failures.



Root Cause Groups:


• 12/24 failures — linuxptp-daemon log stream unreachable (ClockSync GetProfileLogID/GetClockIDForeign timeout): All ClockSync tests in bc (3), dualfollower (3), dualnicbc (3), dualnicbcha (2), and oc-serial test 1 (1) hit could not find regex in log stream … error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded or timedout waiting for matches. The test helper opens a live kubectl logs --follow stream and waits for a ptp4l profile or sync line that never arrives within 3–10 minutes. Two compounding factors: (a) ptp4l may not be reaching SLAVE state cluster-wide (no grandmaster reachable or log format changed by PR #613), and (b) when the API server began degrading, streaming connections were silently severed rather than cleanly refused.

• 10/24 failures — Kubernetes API server Unauthorized mid-run (oc-serial tests 2–11): After the first oc-serial test completed at ~07:29 UTC, the kubeconfig credentials expired or the API server rejected them (Unauthorized / couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials). Every subsequent test aborted immediately at the DaemonSet readiness check (linuxptp-daemon DaemonSet must exist before readiness wait → Unauthorized). One variant also showed dial tcp 10.8.34.117:6443: connection refused, indicating the API server briefly went fully unreachable.

• 2/24 failures — TGM holdover state-machine regression (tgm suite): Both T-GM holdover tests fail deterministically: disabling GNSS causes ts2phcState to be forced to PTP_FREERUN (not PTP_HOLDOVER) when NoSourceTSCount == 2 in linuxptp-daemon/pkg/event/event.go:402–403, making ClockClass 7 unreachable. Test 1 exhausted its 3-minute poll; test 2 observed map[metric:248] (freerun) throughout the 10-second sampling window. This is a deterministic regression, not a flake.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


PatternSuites affected
GetProfileLogID / GetClockIDForeign log stream timeoutbc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, oc (test 1)
API server Unauthorized cascadeoc (tests 2–11)
GNSS → ClockClass 7 holdover state-machine bugtgm

The log-stream failures are 100% cross-suite (5 of 6 suites, identical error text, different pod names) — confirming a cluster-wide cause rather than a suite-specific issue. The oc Unauthorized failures are oc-specific but are a secondary consequence of the same cluster degradation event. The tgm failures are suite-specific and code-driven.


No suite is entirely clean. dualnicbcha is the only suite where the log-stream failure count is below its total suite size (2 of 2 failed; no "passing" baseline in that suite either).



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Re-run the job immediately and monitor the PTP grandmaster. The log-stream and API-auth failures bear all hallmarks of a transient lab infrastructure event (grandmaster unreachable, credential rotation, API server instability on telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com around 2026-06-24 07:00–08:30 UTC). A clean re-run will confirm whether the 22 non-TGM failures are infra flakes.

2. Audit linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (0459bc0, "upstream-sync-2026-06-22") for ptp4l log-format changes. If the upstream sync altered the format of the Ptp4lConf: or selected best master clock log lines, the test regexes in ptphelper.go and pods.go will never match regardless of cluster health. Compare the ptp4l log output from a pre-PR-613 build against a post-PR-613 pod to confirm or rule out a log-format regression.

3. Fix the TGM holdover state machine in event.go:402–403. Remove or guard the unconditional ts2phcState = PTP_FREERUN override when NoSourceTSCount == 2; instead, allow the state to transition to PTP_HOLDOVER so the existing ClockClass 7 branch at lines 459–461 and 430–433 becomes reachable. This is a deterministic code regression and will not self-heal with a re-run.


Overall Issue Class: infra_other (dominant — 22/24 failures) with an embedded test_regression (2/24 tgm failures from PR #613 state-machine change)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — the entire 24-test job failed; the cluster experienced mid-run auth collapse, and a code regression in the TGM holdover path is deterministic and will recur on every re-run.




Per-Suite Details



bc



Suite: bc (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 ClockSync tests failed because Kubernetes log streaming to linuxptp-daemon pods returned context deadline exceeded / timeout errors during the GetProfileLogID step, preventing the test from locating the ptp4l config log line needed to derive the foreign master clock ID.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Kubernetes pod log stream failure in GetProfileLogID (affects: all 3 tests — "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-hrsj8 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=timedout waiting for matches


could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-fpgx7 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

From the detailed agent logs (prior identical run same day, pod linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg):

```

[ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 1 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...

[ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 2 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...

[ptptesthelper.go: 68] GetClockIDForeign retry due to err: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

Additionally, the API server was returning Unauthorized by 08:29 UTC (end of job), with the bc ClockSync tests running at 07:02–08:29 UTC — indicating progressive auth/credential degradation throughout the long 3.5-hour job:

```

error: You must be logged in to the server (Unauthorized)

E0624 08:29:47.118913 memcache.go:265] "Unhandled Error" err="couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials"

```

• Root Cause: The test helper GetProfileLogID in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go opens a live Kubernetes log stream (Follow: true, no TailLines) via pods.GetPodLogsRegex searching for the regex (?m).?Ptp4lConf: #profile: (.|\n)?message_tag \[([^\]]*)\] within a 3-minute timeout. The stream — opened with context.TODO() so no test-side deadline — is being terminated by the infrastructure with context deadline exceeded before the regex can be matched. This error pattern repeats identically across all ClockSync tests in bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, and dualnicbcha suites, confirming a cluster-wide log streaming connectivity issue rather than a daemon log format regression. The linuxptp-daemon does emit this log line (printNodeProfile in pkg/daemon/daemon.go:1369 writes the rendered Ptp4lConf with #profile: prefix and message_tag), but the HTTP connection carrying the pod log stream is dropped before the data reaches the test. The progressive Unauthorized failures at job end suggest the kubeconfig token was near expiry, which may also have disrupted API server proxy connections to node kubelets mid-run.

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-hrsj8 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=timedout waiting for matches


could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-fpgx7 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

From the detailed agent logs (prior identical run same day, pod linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg):

```

[ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 1 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...

[ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 2 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...

[ptptesthelper.go: 68] GetClockIDForeign retry due to err: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

Additionally, the API server was returning Unauthorized by 08:29 UTC (end of job), with the bc ClockSync tests running at 07:02–08:29 UTC — indicating progressive auth/credential degradation throughout the long 3.5-hour job:

```

error: You must be logged in to the server (Unauthorized)

E0624 08:29:47.118913 memcache.go:265] "Unhandled Error" err="couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials"

```

• Root Cause: The test helper GetProfileLogID in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go opens a live Kubernetes log stream (Follow: true, no TailLines) via pods.GetPodLogsRegex searching for the regex (?m).?Ptp4lConf: #profile: (.|\n)?message_tag \[([^\]]*)\] within a 3-minute timeout. The stream — opened with context.TODO() so no test-side deadline — is being terminated by the infrastructure with context deadline exceeded before the regex can be matched. This error pattern repeats identically across all ClockSync tests in bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, and dualnicbcha suites, confirming a cluster-wide log streaming connectivity issue rather than a daemon log format regression. The linuxptp-daemon does emit this log line (printNodeProfile in pkg/daemon/daemon.go:1369 writes the rendered Ptp4lConf with #profile: prefix and message_tag), but the HTTP connection carrying the pod log stream is dropped before the data reaches the test. The progressive Unauthorized failures at job end suggest the kubeconfig token was near expiry, which may also have disrupted API server proxy connections to node kubelets mid-run.
• Breaking PR/Commit:


TL;DR: Cluster log-stream collapse + API auth expiry wiped 22 of 24 tests; a linuxptp-daemon state-machine regression caused the remaining 2 TGM holdover failures.



Root Cause Groups:


• 12/24 failures — linuxptp-daemon log stream unreachable (ClockSync GetProfileLogID/GetClockIDForeign timeout): All ClockSync tests in bc (3), dualfollower (3), dualnicbc (3), dualnicbcha (2), and oc-serial test 1 (1) hit could not find regex in log stream … error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded or timedout waiting for matches. The test helper opens a live kubectl logs --follow stream and waits for a ptp4l profile or sync line that never arrives within 3–10 minutes. Two compounding factors: (a) ptp4l may not be reaching SLAVE state cluster-wide (no grandmaster reachable or log format changed by PR #613), and (b) when the API server began degrading, streaming connections were silently severed rather than cleanly refused.

• 10/24 failures — Kubernetes API server Unauthorized mid-run (oc-serial tests 2–11): After the first oc-serial test completed at ~07:29 UTC, the kubeconfig credentials expired or the API server rejected them (Unauthorized / couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials). Every subsequent test aborted immediately at the DaemonSet readiness check (linuxptp-daemon DaemonSet must exist before readiness wait → Unauthorized). One variant also showed dial tcp 10.8.34.117:6443: connection refused, indicating the API server briefly went fully unreachable.

• 2/24 failures — TGM holdover state-machine regression (tgm suite): Both T-GM holdover tests fail deterministically: disabling GNSS causes ts2phcState to be forced to PTP_FREERUN (not PTP_HOLDOVER) when NoSourceTSCount == 2 in linuxptp-daemon/pkg/event/event.go:402–403, making ClockClass 7 unreachable. Test 1 exhausted its 3-minute poll; test 2 observed map[metric:248] (freerun) throughout the 10-second sampling window. This is a deterministic regression, not a flake.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


PatternSuites affected
GetProfileLogID / GetClockIDForeign log stream timeoutbc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, oc (test 1)
API server Unauthorized cascadeoc (tests 2–11)
GNSS → ClockClass 7 holdover state-machine bugtgm

The log-stream failures are 100% cross-suite (5 of 6 suites, identical error text, different pod names) — confirming a cluster-wide cause rather than a suite-specific issue. The oc Unauthorized failures are oc-specific but are a secondary consequence of the same cluster degradation event. The tgm failures are suite-specific and code-driven.


No suite is entirely clean. dualnicbcha is the only suite where the log-stream failure count is below its total suite size (2 of 2 failed; no "passing" baseline in that suite either).



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Re-run the job immediately and monitor the PTP grandmaster. The log-stream and API-auth failures bear all hallmarks of a transient lab infrastructure event (grandmaster unreachable, credential rotation, API server instability on telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com around 2026-06-24 07:00–08:30 UTC). A clean re-run will confirm whether the 22 non-TGM failures are infra flakes.

2. Audit linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (0459bc0, "upstream-sync-2026-06-22") for ptp4l log-format changes. If the upstream sync altered the format of the Ptp4lConf: or selected best master clock log lines, the test regexes in ptphelper.go and pods.go will never match regardless of cluster health. Compare the ptp4l log output from a pre-PR-613 build against a post-PR-613 pod to confirm or rule out a log-format regression.

3. Fix the TGM holdover state machine in event.go:402–403. Remove or guard the unconditional ts2phcState = PTP_FREERUN override when NoSourceTSCount == 2; instead, allow the state to transition to PTP_HOLDOVER so the existing ClockClass 7 branch at lines 459–461 and 430–433 becomes reachable. This is a deterministic code regression and will not self-heal with a re-run.


Overall Issue Class: infra_other (dominant — 22/24 failures) with an embedded test_regression (2/24 tgm failures from PR #613 state-machine change)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — the entire 24-test job failed; the cluster experienced mid-run auth collapse, and a code regression in the TGM holdover path is deterministic and will recur on every re-run.




Per-Suite Details



bc



Suite: bc (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 ClockSync tests failed because Kubernetes log streaming to linuxptp-daemon pods returned context deadline exceeded / timeout errors during the GetProfileLogID step, preventing the test from locating the ptp4l config log line needed to derive the foreign master clock ID.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Kubernetes pod log stream failure in GetProfileLogID (affects: all 3 tests — "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-hrsj8 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=timedout waiting for matches


could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-fpgx7 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

From the detailed agent logs (prior identical run same day, pod linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg):

```

[ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 1 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...

[ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 2 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...

[ptptesthelper.go: 68] GetClockIDForeign retry due to err: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

Additionally, the API server was returning Unauthorized by 08:29 UTC (end of job), with the bc ClockSync tests running at 07:02–08:29 UTC — indicating progressive auth/credential degradation throughout the long 3.5-hour job:

```

error: You must be logged in to the server (Unauthorized)

E0624 08:29:47.118913 memcache.go:265] "Unhandled Error" err="couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials"

The stream — opened with context.TODO() so no test-side deadline — is being terminated by the infrastructure with context deadline exceeded before the regex can be matched. This error pattern repeats identically across all ClockSync tests in bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, and dualnicbcha suites, confirming a cluster-wide log streaming connectivity issue rather than a daemon log format regression. The linuxptp-daemon does emit this log line (printNodeProfile in pkg/daemon/daemon.go:1369 writes the rendered Ptp4lConf with #profile: prefix and message_tag), but the HTTP connection carrying the pod log stream is dropped before the data reaches the test. The progressive Unauthorized failures at job end suggest the kubeconfig token was near expiry, which may also have disrupted API server proxy connections to node kubelets mid-run.

• Breaking PR/Commit:



Cross-Suite Summary: periodic-ci-openshift-release-main-nightly-4.19-e2e-telco5g-ptp


Job: periodic-ci-openshift-release-main-nightly-4.19-e2e-telco5g-ptp | Version: 4.19 | Total failures: 21



TL;DR: 19 of 21 failures trace to progressive API server credential expiry; 2 are a likely hardware flake in the TGM suite.



Root Cause Groups:


• 19/21 failures — Kubeconfig credential expiry / API server degradation (suites: bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha)

The ~3.5-hour serial run exhausted the cluster's short-lived service-account token. Degradation progressed in three observable stages across suites:

1. Early degradation (dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha): Kubernetes log-stream connections to linuxptp-daemon-* pods dropped with error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded or timedout waiting for matches, preventing the test helper (ptphelper.go:631/811) from confirming PTP sync via regex match on container log output.
2. Full TCP unreachability (bc, first failure): The control-plane VIP 10.8.34.117:6443 became TCP-unreachable mid-suite (dial tcp … i/o timeout), timing out after 573 s.
3. Hard 401 Unauthorized cascade (bc, 9 remaining + post-test gather): After the VIP came back, every API call was rejected with Unauthorized — consistent with a control-plane restart triggering token rotation — cascading through all remaining bc tests and all post-test artifact collection steps.

• 2/21 failures — WPC T-GM DPLL never entered HOLDOVER within test window (suite: tgm, suite-specific)

The first tgm test ("Testing WPC T-GM holdover through connection loss") timed out after ~59.5 minutes waiting for clock class 7. The dpll.go:650–674 state machine requires hardware DPLL state 4 (DPLL_HOLDOVER) before setting clock class 7; that transition never occurred within the polling window, exhausting the suite budget and preventing the second test from running at all (pure cascade, A suite timeout occurred). A known TODO at dpll.go:656 flags instability in this GNSS holdover path. No daemon logs from the failing run are available to distinguish a hardware/timing flake from a daemon regression.



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causebcdualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
API credential expiry / log-stream timeout✓ (11)✓ (3)✓ (3)✓ (2)—
TGM DPLL holdover hardware/timing failure————✓ (2)

The API credential failure is fully cross-suite (4 of 5 suites). The context deadline exceeded log-stream pattern appears in identical form across dualfollower, dualnicbc, and dualnicbcha on different daemon pods (vzwc2, fv8pz, 2sffq, 4nzrs, 2h2w9), confirming it is a cluster-wide event and not pod-specific. The bc suite experienced the most severe form (full TCP timeout then 401 cascade) because it ran later in the job when credential state was furthest degraded. The tgm failure is suite-specific and unrelated to credentials — it involves hardware GNSS/DPLL behavior on cnfdf30/31/32.



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Re-run the job immediately. All 19 API-credential failures are consistent flakes caused by token expiry mid-run; a clean re-run on a freshly provisioned cluster (fresh credentials) should clear them. If the same Unauthorized cascade recurs, investigate the ptpcimno cluster's kubeconfig refresh mechanism and whether the telco5g CI credential vault rotates tokens during the run window.

2. Fix credential lifetime / mid-run refresh for long serial runs. The root cause is structural: a ~3.5-hour serial suite that outlives the service-account token TTL. Either (a) increase the token TTL for the CI service account on ptpcimno, (b) implement a credential refresh step between serial suites, or (c) break up the serial suite chain so each segment gets a fresh kubeconfig. The dualfollower/dualnicbc/dualnicbcha degradation before the hard bc TCP failure suggests the window of failure is predictable and fixable.

3. Capture linuxptp-daemon logs for the tgm holdover test on the next run. The 2 tgm failures cannot be classified as flake vs. regression without daemon output showing the DPLL state transitions. Add log capture for linuxptp-daemon pods on cnfdf30/31/32 nodes scoped to the holdover test window. If the DPLL never emits state 4, the issue is the GNSS simulation setup or hardware; if it emits state 4 but clock class 7 is still not set, investigate the isInSpecOffsetInRange() check at dpll.go:868 and the known tech-debt at dpll.go:656.


Overall Issue Class: infra_timeout


Overall Severity: HIGH — 19/21 failures are a systemic infra credential issue that will repeat every long run; the tgm path has a known flaky hardware dependency that blocks that suite entirely when it occurs.




Per-Suite Details



bc



Suite: bc (11 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 11 bc-suite failures stem from a cluster-level API server disruption mid-run — the control plane became unreachable (i/o timeout) and then began rejecting credentials (HTTP 401 Unauthorized), cascading into every subsequent test timing out.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: API Server TCP Unreachable (affects: Slave can sync to master)
• Evidence:

```

finding pod for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: Get

"https://10.8.34.117:6443/api/v1/namespaces/openshift-ptp/pods?labelSelector=app%3Dlinuxptp-daemon":

dial tcp 10.8.34.117:6443: i/o timeout

```

This test timed out after 573.498 s — it kept retrying until it hit the Ginkgo deadline.

• Root Cause: The API server endpoint at 10.8.34.117:6443 (a VIP or floating-IP for the control plane, distinct from the master node IPs 10.8.34.105/106/110 listed in oc get nodes) became TCP-unreachable mid-test. The test helper GetForeignClockID (calls into ptphelper.go, line ~631) polls pod logs via the API; with no API connectivity it spins until timeout. No cluster node was in NotReady state when the suite started, so this is a transient control-plane disruption that began after the suite was already running.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — infra disruption, not a code change.
• Is it a flake? Yes
• Suggested Fix: No code change needed; re-run the job. If this IP (10.8.34.117) recurs, verify the telco5g API VIP health and the control-plane load balancer for the ptpcimno cluster.

• Group 2: API Server Returning HTTP 401 Unauthorized (affects: 9 remaining tests + suite timeout)
• Evidence:

```

linuxptp-daemon DaemonSet must exist before readiness wait

Expected success, but got an error:

<*errors.StatusError | 0xc000999720>:

Unauthorized

...

Status: "Failure",

Message: "Unauthorized

```

Repeated identically for 9 consecutive tests. Post-test artifact gather confirms the same state:

```

error: You must be logged in to the server (Unauthorized)

error running backup collection: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials

creating temp namespace: Unauthorized

```

And from the step entrypoint:

```

error: You must be logged in to the server (Unauthorized)

{"component":"entrypoint","error":"wrapped process failed: exit status 1",...}

```

• Root Cause: After the control plane disruption in Group 1, every subsequent test fails at its first API call — checking whether the linuxptp-daemon DaemonSet exists in openshift-ptp. The cluster API came back but is rejecting the KUBECONFIG credentials (short-lived service-account token or client certificate was invalidated, e.g., due to a control-plane restart triggering token rotation). All 9 tests hit their 300 s Ginkgo timeout doing this single failing call. The final test receives A suite timeout occurred because the 1-hour suite timer expired. The artifact-gathering pods (`must-gat
2026-07-02T05:00

Root Cause Groups:


• 12/24 failures — linuxptp-daemon log stream unreachable (ClockSync GetProfileLogID/GetClockIDForeign timeout): All ClockSync tests in bc (3), dualfollower (3), dualnicbc (3), dualnicbcha (2), and oc-serial test 1 (1) hit could not find regex in log stream … error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded or timedout waiting for matches. The test helper opens a live kubectl logs --follow stream and waits for a ptp4l profile or sync line that never arrives within 3–10 minutes. Two compounding factors: (a) ptp4l may not be reaching SLAVE state cluster-wide (no grandmaster reachable or log format changed by PR #613), and (b) when the API server began degrading, streaming connections were silently severed rather than cleanly refused.

• 10/24 failures — Kubernetes API server Unauthorized mid-run (oc-serial tests 2–11): After the first oc-serial test completed at ~07:29 UTC, the kubeconfig credentials expired or the API server rejected them (Unauthorized / couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials). Every subsequent test aborted immediately at the DaemonSet readiness check (linuxptp-daemon DaemonSet must exist before readiness wait → Unauthorized). One variant also showed dial tcp 10.8.34.117:6443: connection refused, indicating the API server briefly went fully unreachable.

• 2/24 failures — TGM holdover state-machine regression (tgm suite): Both T-GM holdover tests fail deterministically: disabling GNSS causes ts2phcState to be forced to PTP_FREERUN (not PTP_HOLDOVER) when NoSourceTSCount == 2 in linuxptp-daemon/pkg/event/event.go:402–403, making ClockClass 7 unreachable. Test 1 exhausted its 3-minute poll; test 2 observed map[metric:248] (freerun) throughout the 10-second sampling window. This is a deterministic regression, not a flake.

GetProfileLogID / GetClockIDForeign log stream timeoutbc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, oc (test 1)
API server Unauthorized cascadeoc (tests 2–11)

The oc Unauthorized failures are oc-specific but are a secondary consequence of the same cluster degradation event. The tgm failures are suite-specific and code-driven.

2. Audit linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (0459bc0, "upstream-sync-2026-06-22") for ptp4l log-format changes. If the upstream sync altered the format of the Ptp4lConf: or selected best master clock log lines, the test regexes in ptphelper.go and pods.go will never match regardless of cluster health. Compare the ptp4l log output from a pre-PR-613 build against a post-PR-613 pod to confirm or rule out a log-format regression.

3. Fix the TGM holdover state machine in event.go:402–403. Remove or guard the unconditional ts2phcState = PTP_FREERUN override when NoSourceTSCount == 2; instead, allow the state to transition to PTP_HOLDOVER so the existing ClockClass 7 branch at lines 459–461 and 430–433 becomes reachable. This is a deterministic code regression and will not self-heal with a re-run.


Overall Issue Class: infra_other (dominant — 22/24 failures) with an embedded test_regression (2/24 tgm failures from PR #613 state-machine change)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — the entire 24-test job failed; the cluster experienced mid-run auth collapse, and a code regression in the TGM holdover path is deterministic and will recur on every re-run.




Per-Suite Details



bc



Suite: bc (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 ClockSync tests failed because Kubernetes log streaming to linuxptp-daemon pods returned context deadline exceeded / timeout errors during the GetProfileLogID step, preventing the test from locating the ptp4l config log line needed to derive the foreign master clock ID.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Kubernetes pod log stream failure in GetProfileLogID (affects: all 3 tests — "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-hrsj8 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=timedout waiting for matches


could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-fpgx7 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

From the detailed agent logs (prior identical run same day, pod linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg):

```

[ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 1 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...

[ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 2 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...

[ptptesthelper.go: 68] GetClockIDForeign retry due to err: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

Additionally, the API server was returning Unauthorized by 08:29 UTC (end of job), with the bc ClockSync tests running at 07:02–08:29 UTC — indicating progressive auth/credential degradation throughout the long 3.5-hour job:

```

error: You must be logged in to the server (Unauthorized)

E0624 08:29:47.118913 memcache.go:265] "Unhandled Error" err="couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials"

```

• Root Cause: The test helper GetProfileLogID in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go opens a live Kubernetes log stream (Follow: true, no TailLines) via pods.GetPodLogsRegex searching for the regex (?m).?Ptp4lConf: #profile: (.|\n)?message_tag \[([^\]]*)\] within a 3-minute timeout. The stream — opened with context.TODO() so no test-side deadline — is being terminated by the infrastructure with context deadline exceeded before the regex can be matched. This error pattern repeats identically across all ClockSync tests in bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, and dualnicbcha suites, confirming a cluster-wide log streaming connectivity issue rather than a daemon log format regression. The linuxptp-daemon does emit this log line (printNodeProfile in pkg/daemon/daemon.go:1369 writes the rendered Ptp4lConf with #profile: prefix and message_tag), but the HTTP connection carrying the pod log stream is dropped before the data reaches the test. The progressive Unauthorized failures at job end suggest the kubeconfig token was near expiry, which may also have disrupted API server proxy connections to node kubelets mid-run.
• Breaking PR/Commit:


TL;DR: Cluster log-stream collapse + API auth expiry wiped 22 of 24 tests; a linuxptp-daemon state-machine regression caused the remaining 2 TGM holdover failures.



Root Cause Groups:


• 12/24 failures — linuxptp-daemon log stream unreachable (ClockSync GetProfileLogID/GetClockIDForeign timeout): All ClockSync tests in bc (3), dualfollower (3), dualnicbc (3), dualnicbcha (2), and oc-serial test 1 (1) hit could not find regex in log stream … error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded or timedout waiting for matches. The test helper opens a live kubectl logs --follow stream and waits for a ptp4l profile or sync line that never arrives within 3–10 minutes. Two compounding factors: (a) ptp4l may not be reaching SLAVE state cluster-wide (no grandmaster reachable or log format changed by PR #613), and (b) when the API server began degrading, streaming connections were silently severed rather than cleanly refused.

• 10/24 failures — Kubernetes API server Unauthorized mid-run (oc-serial tests 2–11): After the first oc-serial test completed at ~07:29 UTC, the kubeconfig credentials expired or the API server rejected them (Unauthorized / couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials). Every subsequent test aborted immediately at the DaemonSet readiness check (linuxptp-daemon DaemonSet must exist before readiness wait → Unauthorized). One variant also showed dial tcp 10.8.34.117:6443: connection refused, indicating the API server briefly went fully unreachable.

• 2/24 failures — TGM holdover state-machine regression (tgm suite): Both T-GM holdover tests fail deterministically: disabling GNSS causes ts2phcState to be forced to PTP_FREERUN (not PTP_HOLDOVER) when NoSourceTSCount == 2 in linuxptp-daemon/pkg/event/event.go:402–403, making ClockClass 7 unreachable. Test 1 exhausted its 3-minute poll; test 2 observed map[metric:248] (freerun) throughout the 10-second sampling window. This is a deterministic regression, not a flake.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


PatternSuites affected
GetProfileLogID / GetClockIDForeign log stream timeoutbc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, oc (test 1)
API server Unauthorized cascadeoc (tests 2–11)
GNSS → ClockClass 7 holdover state-machine bugtgm

The log-stream failures are 100% cross-suite (5 of 6 suites, identical error text, different pod names) — confirming a cluster-wide cause rather than a suite-specific issue. The oc Unauthorized failures are oc-specific but are a secondary consequence of the same cluster degradation event. The tgm failures are suite-specific and code-driven.


No suite is entirely clean. dualnicbcha is the only suite where the log-stream failure count is below its total suite size (2 of 2 failed; no "passing" baseline in that suite either).



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Re-run the job immediately and monitor the PTP grandmaster. The log-stream and API-auth failures bear all hallmarks of a transient lab infrastructure event (grandmaster unreachable, credential rotation, API server instability on telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com around 2026-06-24 07:00–08:30 UTC). A clean re-run will confirm whether the 22 non-TGM failures are infra flakes.

2. Audit linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (0459bc0, "upstream-sync-2026-06-22") for ptp4l log-format changes. If the upstream sync altered the format of the Ptp4lConf: or selected best master clock log lines, the test regexes in ptphelper.go and pods.go will never match regardless of cluster health. Compare the ptp4l log output from a pre-PR-613 build against a post-PR-613 pod to confirm or rule out a log-format regression.

3. Fix the TGM holdover state machine in event.go:402–403. Remove or guard the unconditional ts2phcState = PTP_FREERUN override when NoSourceTSCount == 2; instead, allow the state to transition to PTP_HOLDOVER so the existing ClockClass 7 branch at lines 459–461 and 430–433 becomes reachable. This is a deterministic code regression and will not self-heal with a re-run.


Overall Issue Class: infra_other (dominant — 22/24 failures) with an embedded test_regression (2/24 tgm failures from PR #613 state-machine change)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — the entire 24-test job failed; the cluster experienced mid-run auth collapse, and a code regression in the TGM holdover path is deterministic and will recur on every re-run.

A clean re-run will confirm whether the 22 non-TGM failures are infra flakes.

2. Audit linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (0459bc0, "upstream-sync-2026-06-22") for ptp4l log-format changes. If the upstream sync altered the format of the Ptp4lConf: or selected best master clock log lines, the test regexes in ptphelper.go and pods.go will never match regardless of cluster health. Compare the ptp4l log output from a pre-PR-613 build against a post-PR-613 pod to confirm or rule out a log-format regression.

3. Fix the TGM holdover state machine in event.go:402–403. Remove or guard the unconditional ts2phcState = PTP_FREERUN override when NoSourceTSCount == 2; instead, allow the state to transition to PTP_HOLDOVER so the existing ClockClass 7 branch at lines 459–461 and 430–433 becomes reachable. This is a deterministic code regression and will not self-heal with a re-run.


Overall Issue Class: infra_other (dominant — 22/24 failures) with an embedded test_regression (2/24 tgm failures from PR #613 state-machine change)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — the entire 24-test job failed; the cluster experienced mid-run auth collapse, and a code regression in the TGM holdover path is deterministic and will recur on every re-run.




Per-Suite Details



bc



Suite: bc (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 ClockSync tests failed because Kubernetes log streaming to linuxptp-daemon pods returned context deadline exceeded / timeout errors during the GetProfileLogID step, preventing the test from locating the ptp4l config log line needed to derive the foreign master clock ID.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Kubernetes pod log stream failure in GetProfileLogID (affects: all 3 tests — "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-hrsj8 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=timedout waiting for matches


could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-fpgx7 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

From the detailed agent logs (prior identical run same day, pod linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg):

```

[ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 1 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...

[ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 2 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...

[ptptesthelper.go: 68] GetClockIDForeign retry due to err: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

Additionally, the API server was returning Unauthorized by 08:29 UTC (end of job), with the bc ClockSync tests running at 07:02–08:29 UTC — indicating progressive auth/credential degradation throughout the long 3.5-hour job:

```

error: You must be logged in to the server (Unauthorized)

E0624 08:29:47.118913 memcache.go:265] "Unhandled Error" err="couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials"

```

• Root Cause: The test helper GetProfileLogID in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go opens a live Kubernetes log stream (Follow: true, no TailLines) via pods.GetPodLogsRegex searching for the regex (?m).?Ptp4lConf: #profile: (.|\n)?message_tag \[([^\]]*)\] within a 3-minute timeout. The stream — opened with context.TODO() so no test-side deadline — is being terminated by the infrastructure with context deadline exceeded before the regex can be matched. This error pattern repeats identically across all ClockSync tests in bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, and dualnicbcha suites, confirming a cluster-wide log streaming connectivity issue rather than a daemon log format regression. The linuxptp-daemon does emit this log line (printNodeProfile in pkg/daemon/daemon.go:1369 writes the rendered Ptp4lConf with #profile: prefix and message_tag), but the HTTP connection carrying the pod log stream is dropped before the data reaches the test. The progressive Unauthorized failures at job end suggest the kubeconfig token was near expiry, which may also have disrupted API server proxy connections to node kubelets mid-run.
• Breaking PR/Commit:


TL;DR: Cluster log-stream collapse + API auth expiry wiped 22 of 24 tests; a linuxptp-daemon state-machine regression caused the remaining 2 TGM holdover failures.



Root Cause Groups:


• 12/24 failures — linuxptp-daemon log stream unreachable (ClockSync GetProfileLogID/GetClockIDForeign timeout): All ClockSync tests in bc (3), dualfollower (3), dualnicbc (3), dualnicbcha (2), and oc-serial test 1 (1) hit could not find regex in log stream … error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded or timedout waiting for matches. The test helper opens a live kubectl logs --follow stream and waits for a ptp4l profile or sync line that never arrives within 3–10 minutes. Two compounding factors: (a) ptp4l may not be reaching SLAVE state cluster-wide (no grandmaster reachable or log format changed by PR #613), and (b) when the API server began degrading, streaming connections were silently severed rather than cleanly refused.

• 10/24 failures — Kubernetes API server Unauthorized mid-run (oc-serial tests 2–11): After the first oc-serial test completed at ~07:29 UTC, the kubeconfig credentials expired or the API server rejected them (Unauthorized / couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials). Every subsequent test aborted immediately at the DaemonSet readiness check (linuxptp-daemon DaemonSet must exist before readiness wait → Unauthorized). One variant also showed dial tcp 10.8.34.117:6443: connection refused, indicating the API server briefly went fully unreachable.

• 2/24 failures — TGM holdover state-machine regression (tgm suite): Both T-GM holdover tests fail deterministically: disabling GNSS causes ts2phcState to be forced to PTP_FREERUN (not PTP_HOLDOVER) when NoSourceTSCount == 2 in linuxptp-daemon/pkg/event/event.go:402–403, making ClockClass 7 unreachable. Test 1 exhausted its 3-minute poll; test 2 observed map[metric:248] (freerun) throughout the 10-second sampling window. This is a deterministic regression, not a flake.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


PatternSuites affected
GetProfileLogID / GetClockIDForeign log stream timeoutbc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, oc (test 1)
API server Unauthorized cascadeoc (tests 2–11)
GNSS → ClockClass 7 holdover state-machine bugtgm

The log-stream failures are 100% cross-suite (5 of 6 suites, identical error text, different pod names) — confirming a cluster-wide cause rather than a suite-specific issue. The oc Unauthorized failures are oc-specific but are a secondary consequence of the same cluster degradation event. The tgm failures are suite-specific and code-driven.


No suite is entirely clean. dualnicbcha is the only suite where the log-stream failure count is below its total suite size (2 of 2 failed; no "passing" baseline in that suite either).



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Re-run the job immediately and monitor the PTP grandmaster. The log-stream and API-auth failures bear all hallmarks of a transient lab infrastructure event (grandmaster unreachable, credential rotation, API server instability on telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com around 2026-06-24 07:00–08:30 UTC). A clean re-run will confirm whether the 22 non-TGM failures are infra flakes.

2. Audit linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (0459bc0, "upstream-sync-2026-06-22") for ptp4l log-format changes. If the upstream sync altered the format of the Ptp4lConf: or selected best master clock log lines, the test regexes in ptphelper.go and pods.go will never match regardless of cluster health. Compare the ptp4l log output from a pre-PR-613 build against a post-PR-613 pod to confirm or rule out a log-format regression.

3. Fix the TGM holdover state machine in event.go:402–403. Remove or guard the unconditional ts2phcState = PTP_FREERUN override when NoSourceTSCount == 2; instead, allow the state to transition to PTP_HOLDOVER so the existing ClockClass 7 branch at lines 459–461 and 430–433 becomes reachable. This is a deterministic code regression and will not self-heal with a re-run.


Overall Issue Class: infra_other (dominant — 22/24 failures) with an embedded test_regression (2/24 tgm failures from PR #613 state-machine change)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — the entire 24-test job failed; the cluster experienced mid-run auth collapse, and a code regression in the TGM holdover path is deterministic and will recur on every re-run.

No suite is entirely clean. dualnicbcha is the only suite where the log-stream failure count is below its total suite size (2 of 2 failed; no "passing" baseline in that suite either).



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Re-run the job immediately and monitor the PTP grandmaster. The log-stream and API-auth failures bear all hallmarks of a transient lab infrastructure event (grandmaster unreachable, credential rotation, API server instability on telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com around 2026-06-24 07:00–08:30 UTC). A clean re-run will confirm whether the 22 non-TGM failures are infra flakes.

2. Audit linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (0459bc0, "upstream-sync-2026-06-22") for ptp4l log-format changes. If the upstream sync altered the format of the Ptp4lConf: or selected best master clock log lines, the test regexes in ptphelper.go and pods.go will never match regardless of cluster health. Compare the ptp4l log output from a pre-PR-613 build against a post-PR-613 pod to confirm or rule out a log-format regression.

3. Fix the TGM holdover state machine in event.go:402–403. Remove or guard the unconditional ts2phcState = PTP_FREERUN override when NoSourceTSCount == 2; instead, allow the state to transition to PTP_HOLDOVER so the existing ClockClass 7 branch at lines 459–461 and 430–433 becomes reachable. This is a deterministic code regression and will not self-heal with a re-run.


Overall Issue Class: infra_other (dominant — 22/24 failures) with an embedded test_regression (2/24 tgm failures from PR #613 state-machine change)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — the entire 24-test job failed; the cluster experienced mid-run auth collapse, and a code regression in the TGM holdover path is deterministic and will recur on every re-run.




Per-Suite Details



bc



Suite: bc (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 ClockSync tests failed because Kubernetes log streaming to linuxptp-daemon pods returned context deadline exceeded / timeout errors during the GetProfileLogID step, preventing the test from locating the ptp4l config log line needed to derive the foreign master clock ID.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Kubernetes pod log stream failure in GetProfileLogID (affects: all 3 tests — "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-hrsj8 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=timedout waiting for matches


could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-fpgx7 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

From the detailed agent logs (prior identical run same day, pod linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg):

```

[ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 1 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...

[ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 2 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...

[ptptesthelper.go: 68] GetClockIDForeign retry due to err: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

Additionally, the API server was returning Unauthorized by 08:29 UTC (end of job), with the bc ClockSync tests running at 07:02–08:29 UTC — indicating progressive auth/credential degradation throughout the long 3.5-hour job:

```

error: You must be logged in to the server (Unauthorized)

E0624 08:29:47.118913 memcache.go:265] "Unhandled Error" err="couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials"

```

• Root Cause: The test helper GetProfileLogID in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go opens a live Kubernetes log stream (Follow: true, no TailLines) via pods.GetPodLogsRegex searching for the regex (?m).?Ptp4lConf: #profile: (.|\n)?message_tag \[([^\]]*)\] within a 3-minute timeout. The stream — opened with context.TODO() so no test-side deadline — is being terminated by the infrastructure with context deadline exceeded before the regex can be matched. This error pattern repeats identically across all ClockSync tests in bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, and dualnicbcha suites, confirming a cluster-wide log streaming connectivity issue rather than a daemon log format regression. The linuxptp-daemon does emit this log line (printNodeProfile in pkg/daemon/daemon.go:1369 writes the rendered Ptp4lConf with #profile: prefix and message_tag), but the HTTP connection carrying the pod log stream is dropped before the data reaches the test. The progressive Unauthorized failures at job end suggest the kubeconfig token was near expiry, which may also have disrupted API server proxy connections to node kubelets mid-run.
• Breaking PR/Commit:


TL;DR: Cluster log-stream collapse + API auth expiry wiped 22 of 24 tests; a linuxptp-daemon state-machine regression caused the remaining 2 TGM holdover failures.



Root Cause Groups:


• 12/24 failures — linuxptp-daemon log stream unreachable (ClockSync GetProfileLogID/GetClockIDForeign timeout): All ClockSync tests in bc (3), dualfollower (3), dualnicbc (3), dualnicbcha (2), and oc-serial test 1 (1) hit could not find regex in log stream … error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded or timedout waiting for matches. The test helper opens a live kubectl logs --follow stream and waits for a ptp4l profile or sync line that never arrives within 3–10 minutes. Two compounding factors: (a) ptp4l may not be reaching SLAVE state cluster-wide (no grandmaster reachable or log format changed by PR #613), and (b) when the API server began degrading, streaming connections were silently severed rather than cleanly refused.

• 10/24 failures — Kubernetes API server Unauthorized mid-run (oc-serial tests 2–11): After the first oc-serial test completed at ~07:29 UTC, the kubeconfig credentials expired or the API server rejected them (Unauthorized / couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials). Every subsequent test aborted immediately at the DaemonSet readiness check (linuxptp-daemon DaemonSet must exist before readiness wait → Unauthorized). One variant also showed dial tcp 10.8.34.117:6443: connection refused, indicating the API server briefly went fully unreachable.

• 2/24 failures — TGM holdover state-machine regression (tgm suite): Both T-GM holdover tests fail deterministically: disabling GNSS causes ts2phcState to be forced to PTP_FREERUN (not PTP_HOLDOVER) when NoSourceTSCount == 2 in linuxptp-daemon/pkg/event/event.go:402–403, making ClockClass 7 unreachable. Test 1 exhausted its 3-minute poll; test 2 observed map[metric:248] (freerun) throughout the 10-second sampling window. This is a deterministic regression, not a flake.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


PatternSuites affected
GetProfileLogID / GetClockIDForeign log stream timeoutbc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, oc (test 1)
API server Unauthorized cascadeoc (tests 2–11)
GNSS → ClockClass 7 holdover state-machine bugtgm

The log-stream failures are 100% cross-suite (5 of 6 suites, identical error text, different pod names) — confirming a cluster-wide cause rather than a suite-specific issue. The oc Unauthorized failures are oc-specific but are a secondary consequence of the same cluster degradation event. The tgm failures are suite-specific and code-driven.


No suite is entirely clean. dualnicbcha is the only suite where the log-stream failure count is below its total suite size (2 of 2 failed; no "passing" baseline in that suite either).



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Re-run the job immediately and monitor the PTP grandmaster. The log-stream and API-auth failures bear all hallmarks of a transient lab infrastructure event (grandmaster unreachable, credential rotation, API server instability on telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com around 2026-06-24 07:00–08:30 UTC). A clean re-run will confirm whether the 22 non-TGM failures are infra flakes.

2. Audit linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (0459bc0, "upstream-sync-2026-06-22") for ptp4l log-format changes. If the upstream sync altered the format of the Ptp4lConf: or selected best master clock log lines, the test regexes in ptphelper.go and pods.go will never match regardless of cluster health. Compare the ptp4l log output from a pre-PR-613 build against a post-PR-613 pod to confirm or rule out a log-format regression.

3. Fix the TGM holdover state machine in event.go:402–403. Remove or guard the unconditional ts2phcState = PTP_FREERUN override when NoSourceTSCount == 2; instead, allow the state to transition to PTP_HOLDOVER so the existing ClockClass 7 branch at lines 459–461 and 430–433 becomes reachable. This is a deterministic code regression and will not self-heal with a re-run.


Overall Issue Class: infra_other (dominant — 22/24 failures) with an embedded test_regression (2/24 tgm failures from PR #613 state-machine change)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — the entire 24-test job failed; the cluster experienced mid-run auth collapse, and a code regression in the TGM holdover path is deterministic and will recur on every re-run.
